title: Cosmos deficiens
intro: Cosmos deficiens, also known as Purple Vervain, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Americas and used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which provide health benefits such as relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Cosmos deficiens include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in aromatherapy and as a natural insect repellent.
taxonomy: Cosmos deficiens is classified as a species within the genus Cosmos, which is a part of the family Asteraceae. Cosmos deficiens is also known as the Bird's Eye Cosmos or Pink Dyer's Cosmos. It belongs to the order Asterales. Within the classification, Cosmos deficiens is placed in the kingdom Plantae and phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Cosmos deficiens is not a commonly recognized scientific name for the cosmos flower. However, Cosmos sulphureus is known to have been used in traditional medicine. Cosmos sulphureus has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues and as an expectorant. Additionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive problems and skin conditions.
